articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Vision India 2047' Blueprint for Economic Transformation
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the comprehensive 'Vision India 2047' document, outlining strategies for India to become a developed nation by its centenary of independence.
• Key pillars include sustainable development, digital public infrastructure expansion, human capital development, and global competitiveness.
• Emphasizes green growth initiatives, clean energy transition, and leveraging technology for inclusive growth.
• Aims for a significant increase in per capita income and a robust manufacturing base.
• Focuses on strengthening federal cooperation for effective implementation across states.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Government Budgeting
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Vision India 2047 aims to make India a developed nation by 2047.
It focuses on green growth, digital economy, and human capital.
The document was approved by the Union Cabinet.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted approach of Vision India 2047 towards achieving developed nation status, including economic, social, and environmental dimensions.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in implementing such an ambitious long-term vision, particularly concerning federalism and resource mobilization.
Evaluate the role of technology and innovation as envisioned in the blueprint for inclusive and sustainable growth.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of India's economic growth path with elements like renewable energy, digital connectivity, and modern infrastructure.
articles.trivia: India's first five-year plan, launched in 1951, focused primarily on agricultural development.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economic and Social Development
prelimsSyllabus.area: Sustainable Development, Poverty, Inclusion, Demographics, Social Sector Initiatives
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors

articles.headline: India Hosts 3rd Quad Leaders' Summit, Reinforcing Indo-Pacific Security and Tech Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• Leaders from India, the United States, Japan, and Australia convened in New Delhi for the third in-person Quad Summit.
• Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in maritime security, counter-terrorism, critical and emerging technologies, and resilient supply chains.
• A new 'Quad Infrastructure Partnership' was announced to support sustainable and transparent infrastructure development in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Emphasized commitment to a free, open, inclusive, and resilient Indo-Pacific, upholding international law and norms.
• Agreed on joint initiatives for climate action, health security, and humanitarian assistance/disaster relief.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: The 3rd Quad Leaders' Summit was hosted by India in New Delhi.
Quad members include India, USA, Japan, and Australia.
A 'Quad Infrastructure Partnership' was announced.
Focus areas include maritime security, critical technologies, and climate action.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Quad grouping for India's strategic interests in the Indo-Pacific region and its role in global governance.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for the Quad in balancing security concerns with economic cooperation and regional inclusivity.
Evaluate the potential impact of the 'Quad Infrastructure Partnership' on regional development and geopolitical dynamics.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders of the Quad nations shaking hands at the summit venue in New Delhi.
articles.trivia: The idea of the Quad was first mooted by Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe in 2007.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: International Institutions, agencies and fora, their structure and mandate
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Important International institutions, agencies and fora – their structure, mandate

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Targets Revised Upwards Amidst Global Energy Transition Push
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced an upward revision of targets for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming for faster adoption and production.
• The revised target now aims for 10 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) of green hydrogen production capacity by 2030, up from the initial 5 MTPA.
• Incentives for electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen production under the SIGHT program are expected to be further strengthened.
• The move is aimed at positioning India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing significantly to decarbonization goals.
• Emphasizes partnerships with industry and research institutions to drive technological innovation and cost reduction.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission targets for 2030 have been revised to 10 MTPA.
The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production.
SIGHT program provides incentives for electrolyzer manufacturing.
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals, considering the revised targets of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Discuss the challenges in achieving the ambitious green hydrogen production targets, including technological bottlenecks, infrastructure development, and economic viability.
Examine the potential socio-economic benefits and environmental impacts of India's leadership in the global green hydrogen economy.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating the production process of green hydrogen using renewable energy.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Begins Hearing Petitions Challenging Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court commenced hearing a batch of petitions challenging various provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023.
• Petitioners argue that certain clauses infringe upon fundamental rights, particularly the right to privacy and freedom of speech and expression.
• Key points of contention include the wide powers granted to the Data Protection Board, exemptions for government agencies, and the definition of 'personal data'.
• The government is expected to defend the Act, emphasizing its role in safeguarding citizen data and promoting digital economy.
• The outcome of the hearing is crucial for shaping India's data governance framework and digital rights.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
Fundamental Rights
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act was passed in 2023.
The Supreme Court is hearing challenges to the Act.
Arguments revolve around the right to privacy and freedom of speech.
The Data Protection Board is a key institution under the Act.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, that are under judicial scrutiny, particularly concerning the balance between national security and individual privacy.
Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's potential ruling on India's digital economy, data localization, and the rights of digital citizens.
Analyze the role of the Data Protection Board and its independence in safeguarding personal data in India.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology Demonstrator
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone with the successful test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• The test involved a precise landing experiment, simulating the return of a reusable rocket stage from space, crucial for future cost-effective space access.
• This mission validates critical technologies such as autonomous navigation, guidance, and control, and thermal protection systems.
• The RLV program aims to drastically reduce the cost of satellite launches by enabling multiple uses of the first stage of a rocket.
• This success brings India closer to developing its own two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable launch system.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
The test validated autonomous landing and thermal protection systems.
The RLV program aims to reduce satellite launch costs.
It is a step towards India's two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable launch system.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle program for India's future in space exploration and commercial satellite launches.
Discuss the technological challenges involved in developing a fully reusable launch system and how ISRO is addressing them.
Evaluate the economic and geopolitical implications of India achieving cost-effective and reliable access to space through reusable technology.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) descending for a landing.
articles.trivia: The first successful orbital flight of a reusable spacecraft was the Space Shuttle Columbia in 1981.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Interest Rates, Signals Cautious Optimism on Inflation Outlook
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The MPC also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating continued vigilance against inflation.
• RBI Governor stated that while inflation remains a concern, favorable monsoon and supply-side measures could ease price pressures in the coming months.
• Projected GDP growth for FY25 remains robust, supported by strong domestic demand and government capital expenditure.
• Experts suggest the RBI is prioritizing inflation control while carefully monitoring global economic uncertainties before considering any rate cuts.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC kept the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
The stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
The MPC is responsible for setting the benchmark interest rate.
RBI projects robust GDP growth for FY25.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on interest rates and its implications for inflation management and economic growth.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control with supporting economic recovery amidst global uncertainties.
Evaluate the effectiveness of RBI's monetary policy tools in achieving its twin objectives of price stability and sustainable growth.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members – three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.

articles.headline: India and France Conduct Joint Naval Exercise 'Varuna 2024' in Arabian Sea
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy and the French Navy concluded their bilateral maritime exercise 'Varuna 2024' in the Arabian Sea.
• The exercise involved advanced air defence drills, anti-submarine warfare operations, and tactical maneuvers, enhancing interoperability between the two navies.
• Participation included frontline warships, submarines, maritime patrol aircraft, and fighter jets from both sides.
• 'Varuna' is a crucial component of the strategic partnership between India and France, aimed at promoting peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific region.
• The exercise underscored shared commitment to a free, open, and secure maritime domain and addressing common security challenges.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Exercise 'Varuna 2024' is a bilateral naval exercise between India and France.
It was conducted in the Arabian Sea.
The exercise focuses on air defence, anti-submarine warfare, and tactical maneuvers.
It is part of the strategic partnership between the two nations.
mainsPoints: Examine the significance of joint naval exercises like 'Varuna' in strengthening India's maritime security capabilities and fostering strategic partnerships in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss how India-France defense cooperation contributes to regional stability and addresses emerging security challenges in the Indian Ocean Region.
Analyze the role of bilateral military exercises in enhancing interoperability and mutual understanding between armed forces of friendly nations.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and French naval ships conducting maneuvers during Exercise Varuna.
articles.trivia: The 'Varuna' naval exercise between India and France commenced in 1993 and was officially named in 2001.

articles.headline: Ministry of Education Launches 'Skill India Digital 2.0' for Enhanced Vocational Training
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education, in collaboration with the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship, launched 'Skill India Digital 2.0'.
• This upgraded platform aims to provide a comprehensive digital ecosystem for skill development, vocational training, and employment linkages.
• It integrates various government and private sector skilling initiatives, offering personalized learning paths, industry-relevant courses, and digital certifications.
• Focuses on bridging the skill gap in emerging sectors like AI, data science, green jobs, and advanced manufacturing.
• The platform also facilitates direct connection between skilled individuals and employers, enhancing job placement opportunities across the country.
gsSubTopics: Human Resources
Development processes and the development industry – the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Skill India Digital 2.0 is launched by the Ministry of Education and Ministry of Skill Development.
It is a digital ecosystem for skill development and employment.
The platform offers personalized learning paths and digital certifications.
It aims to bridge the skill gap in emerging sectors.
mainsPoints: Discuss how digital platforms like 'Skill India Digital 2.0' can revolutionize vocational training and address the challenges of unemployment and underemployment in India.
Analyze the role of public-private partnerships in making skill development initiatives more effective and industry-relevant.
Examine the potential of 'Skill India Digital 2.0' to foster a culture of lifelong learning and adapt the workforce to future job market demands.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized logo of Skill India Digital with interconnected digital icons.
articles.trivia: The 'Skill India Mission' was launched in 2015 to create convergence across various schemes and programs related to skill training.

articles.headline: Western Ghats Ecological Restoration Project Receives Major International Funding Boost
articles.explainer:
• A large-scale ecological restoration project for the Western Ghats has secured significant funding from international environmental organizations.
• The multi-year project focuses on biodiversity conservation, sustainable land management, and community-based conservation initiatives across the region.
• Key activities include reforestation, invasive species removal, watershed management, and promoting eco-tourism.
• The funding will also support research into climate change impacts on the Western Ghats and adaptation strategies for local communities.
• This initiative underscores the global recognition of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and its crucial role in regional ecology.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is a UNESCO World Heritage site and a biodiversity hotspot.
An ecological restoration project has received international funding.
The project focuses on reforestation, invasive species removal, and watershed management.
It aims to support local communities and climate change adaptation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces from anthropogenic activities and climate change.
Analyze the importance of international cooperation and community participation in successful large-scale ecological restoration projects.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing sustainable land management practices and balancing conservation with development needs in ecologically sensitive regions like the Western Ghats.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with a river flowing through it.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Reforms in Election Funding Transparency
articles.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee submitted its report recommending significant reforms to enhance transparency in election funding in India.
• Key recommendations include bringing all political parties under the ambit of the Right to Information (RTI) Act for greater accountability.
• Suggested capping anonymous donations and mandating disclosure of donors for contributions above a certain threshold.
• The committee also called for strengthening the Election Commission of India's (ECI) powers to regulate political finance and ensure compliance.
• The recommendations aim to address concerns regarding black money in elections and ensure a level playing field for all political parties.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: A parliamentary panel recommended reforms in election funding.
Key recommendations include bringing political parties under RTI Act.
It also suggested capping anonymous donations and strengthening ECI powers.
The aim is to increase transparency in political finance.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the current framework of election funding in India and the challenges it poses to democratic principles and fair elections.
Discuss the feasibility and potential impact of the parliamentary panel's recommendations, such as bringing political parties under the RTI Act, on improving transparency and accountability.
Suggest further reforms that could effectively curb the influence of black money in elections and ensure a level playing field for all political contestants.
articles.imageDescription: Parliament House of India with a ballot box overlay.
articles.trivia: The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body established under Article 324 of the Constitution.

articles.headline: India's Services Sector Records Robust Growth in Q2 2024, Driving Economic Momentum
articles.explainer:
• India's services sector demonstrated strong performance in the second quarter of 2024, significantly contributing to the nation's overall economic growth.
• Key sub-sectors like financial services, IT and IT-enabled services, trade, hotels, and transport showed impressive expansion.
• The growth was underpinned by resilient domestic demand, increased consumption, and recovery in tourism and hospitality.
• This robust performance is crucial for job creation and sustaining the momentum for India's target of becoming a developed economy.
• Government policy support, including digitization initiatives and infrastructure development, played a vital role in enabling this growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Growth and Development
prelimsPoints: India's services sector showed robust growth in Q2 2024.
Financial services, IT, trade, hotels, and transport were key growth drivers.
Domestic demand and tourism recovery contributed to the growth.
The services sector is a significant contributor to India's GDP.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the robust growth of India's services sector and its significance for overall economic development and employment generation.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for sustaining this growth, especially in enhancing productivity, innovation, and global competitiveness of Indian services.
Examine the policy measures required to further leverage the services sector for achieving inclusive growth and making India a developed economy.
articles.imageDescription: Skyline of a modern Indian city representing the services sector.
articles.trivia: The services sector is the largest contributor to India's GDP, accounting for over 50% of the total output.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economic and Social Development
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Components of GDP and economic indicators

articles.headline: India Pledges Substantial Aid to Developing Nations for Climate Resilience at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India announced a significant financial commitment to support climate resilience and adaptation projects in developing countries at a United Nations forum.
• The pledge underscores India's growing leadership in global climate action and its commitment to South-South cooperation.
• Funds will be directed towards initiatives like early warning systems, climate-resilient agriculture, and renewable energy deployment in vulnerable nations.
• The announcement comes ahead of the upcoming COP29, signaling India's proactive stance on climate finance for developing countries.
• Emphasized the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in climate negotiations.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure and mandate
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: India pledged climate aid to developing nations at a UN forum.
The aid focuses on climate resilience and adaptation projects.
It highlights South-South cooperation and India's leadership in climate action.
The principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities (CBDR-RC) was emphasized.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India's climate aid pledge for developing nations in the context of its foreign policy objectives and global climate leadership.
Discuss the challenges in mobilizing and effectively utilizing climate finance for adaptation and resilience in vulnerable countries.
Evaluate India's role in advocating for the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in international climate negotiations.
articles.imageDescription: Globe with hands reaching out, symbolizing international cooperation on climate change.
articles.trivia: The 'Green Climate Fund' (GCF) is a fund established within the framework of the UNFCCC to assist developing countries in adaptation and mitigation practices.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Indigenous Vaccine Development for Neglected Tropical Disease
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientists have announced a significant breakthrough in developing an indigenous vaccine for a prevalent Neglected Tropical Disease (NTD) affecting vulnerable populations.
• The vaccine, developed by a consortium of public and private research institutions, has shown promising results in pre-clinical trials.
• This development is crucial for public health, as NTDs disproportionately affect impoverished communities and often receive limited research funding.
• The 'Make in India' initiative in biotechnology has played a pivotal role in accelerating indigenous research and development.
• Efforts are now underway to scale up production and initiate human clinical trials, aiming for a cost-effective and accessible vaccine.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Health
prelimsPoints: Indian scientists achieved a breakthrough in indigenous vaccine development for a Neglected Tropical Disease (NTD).
The vaccine has shown promising results in pre-clinical trials.
NTDs primarily affect impoverished communities.
The 'Make in India' initiative supports such biotechnology advancements.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous vaccine development for Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) in strengthening India's public health infrastructure and addressing health inequities.
Analyze the role of government policies and public-private partnerships in fostering innovation and self-reliance in the biotechnology and pharmaceutical sectors.
Examine the challenges in bringing new vaccines from research to widespread accessibility, particularly for diseases affecting vulnerable populations.
articles.imageDescription: Microscopic view of a vaccine vial with scientific equipment in the background.
articles.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) currently recognizes 20 diseases as Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs).

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Vaccine development and public health initiatives

articles.headline: New Guidelines Issued for Critical Infrastructure Protection Against Cyber Threats
articles.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) released updated guidelines for safeguarding critical infrastructure from evolving cyber threats.
• The guidelines emphasize a multi-layered approach, including robust security architecture, regular audits, threat intelligence sharing, and incident response mechanisms.
• Focus areas include energy grids, telecommunications networks, financial systems, and defense infrastructure, which are vital for national security and economy.
• The move comes amidst a rising global trend of sophisticated state-sponsored cyber-attacks and ransomware incidents.
• Encourages public-private collaboration and capacity building among cybersecurity professionals to effectively counter cyber warfare.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Money-laundering and its prevention
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.
prelimsPoints: NCIIPC issued new guidelines for critical infrastructure protection.
Guidelines cover energy, telecom, finance, and defense sectors.
They emphasize robust security, audits, and threat intelligence sharing.
NCIIPC stands for National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing menace of cyber threats to India's critical infrastructure and its implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the effectiveness of the new guidelines issued by NCIIPC in mitigating these risks and fostering a resilient cyber ecosystem.
Examine the challenges in implementing comprehensive cybersecurity measures across diverse sectors and the need for greater public-private collaboration and international cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: Digital representation of a protected network infrastructure with a lock icon.
articles.trivia: The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) was established under Section 70A of the Information Technology Act, 2000.

articles.headline: Government Launches Nationwide Campaign to Combat Malnutrition Among Children
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched a comprehensive nationwide campaign, 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan', to address malnutrition among children.
• The campaign focuses on improving nutritional outcomes through targeted interventions, awareness generation, and community participation.
• Initiatives include promoting exclusive breastfeeding, complementary feeding, micronutrient supplementation, and early childhood care.
• Leverages the Anganwadi network and healthcare workers for effective last-mile delivery of services and counseling to mothers.
• Aims to reduce stunting, wasting, and anemia among children under five, aligning with Sustainable Development Goal 2 (Zero Hunger).
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Hunger and poverty
prelimsPoints: The 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan' campaign aims to combat child malnutrition.
It is launched by the Ministry of Women and Child Development.
The campaign focuses on breastfeeding, complementary feeding, and micronutrient supplementation.
It utilizes the Anganwadi network for service delivery.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional causes and consequences of child malnutrition in India and its implications for human capital development and economic growth.
Analyze the effectiveness of government initiatives like 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan' and the Anganwadi system in addressing the challenge of malnutrition.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including inter-sectoral convergence and community engagement, to achieve the Sustainable Development Goal of zero hunger in India.
articles.imageDescription: A healthy child smiling, with a graphic depicting balanced nutrition.
articles.trivia: The 'Poshan Abhiyaan' (National Nutrition Mission) was launched in 2018 to improve nutritional outcomes for children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economic and Social Development
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government schemes for health and nutrition

articles.headline: IMD Issues Advisory for Extreme Weather Events, Emphasizes Disaster Preparedness
articles.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued a nationwide advisory, warning of increased likelihood of extreme weather events in the coming months.
• The advisory highlighted potential for intense rainfall, heatwaves, and localized storms, attributing the trends to ongoing climate change impacts.
• Emphasized the need for robust disaster preparedness measures, including early warning systems, evacuation plans, and resilient infrastructure.
• Urged state governments and disaster management agencies to enhance coordination and public awareness campaigns.
• The IMD also stressed the importance of sustainable urban planning and water management to mitigate the impact of such events.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: IMD issued an advisory on extreme weather events.
It warned of intense rainfall, heatwaves, and localized storms.
The advisory emphasizes disaster preparedness and early warning systems.
IMD is the primary agency for meteorological observations and forecasting in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing frequency and intensity of extreme weather events in India and their attribution to global climate change.
Analyze the effectiveness of India's current disaster preparedness and early warning systems in mitigating the impact of such events.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for enhancing disaster resilience at local and national levels, including infrastructure development, community engagement, and policy reforms.
articles.imageDescription: Weather map showing various extreme weather indicators over India.
articles.trivia: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) was established in 1875 and is an agency of the Ministry of Earth Sciences.

articles.headline: 16th Finance Commission Holds Crucial Meeting on Centre-State Fiscal Relations
articles.explainer:
• The 16th Finance Commission held a significant meeting to deliberate on the framework for horizontal and vertical devolution of taxes between the Centre and States.
• Discussions focused on equitable distribution of resources, addressing fiscal imbalances, and incentivizing states for performance on various parameters.
• The Commission is tasked with making recommendations for the period 2026-2031, impacting state finances and development priorities.
• Key areas under review include criteria for resource sharing, grants-in-aid, and disaster relief funding mechanisms.
• State representatives presented their proposals, advocating for increased fiscal autonomy and greater share in central taxes.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Finance Commission
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: The 16th Finance Commission is deliberating on Centre-State fiscal relations.
It will make recommendations for the period 2026-2031.
The Commission focuses on horizontal and vertical devolution of taxes.
Finance Commission is a constitutional body under Article 280.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional mandate and critical role of the Finance Commission in shaping fiscal federalism in India.
Analyze the key challenges faced by the 16th Finance Commission in ensuring equitable and efficient resource devolution between the Centre and States, considering evolving economic realities.
Evaluate the potential impact of the Commission's recommendations on state finances, development priorities, and the overall trajectory of India's federal structure.
articles.imageDescription: Indian rupee currency notes with a graph showing economic growth, symbolizing fiscal policy.
articles.trivia: The first Finance Commission was constituted in 1951 under the chairmanship of K.C. Neogy.

articles.headline: Government Introduces Amendments to Foreign Trade Policy to Boost Exports and Ease Business
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry announced key amendments to India's Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2023, aimed at boosting exports and simplifying procedures.
• Amendments include rationalization of various export promotion schemes, reducing compliance burden, and promoting e-commerce exports.
• Focus on encouraging 'district as export hub' initiative and supporting MSMEs to participate more actively in global trade.
• The changes are designed to make Indian exports more competitive globally and integrate India further into global value chains.
• Expected to provide significant relief to exporters and facilitate ease of doing business, aligning with the 'Make in India' vision.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Amendments introduced to India's Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2023.
Aims to boost exports and simplify procedures.
Promotes 'district as export hub' and e-commerce exports.
Ministry of Commerce and Industry is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and key features of the recent amendments to India's Foreign Trade Policy in boosting exports and integrating India into global value chains.
Discuss the challenges faced by Indian exporters, particularly MSMEs, in global markets and how these policy changes aim to address them.
Evaluate the potential impact of these amendments on India's trade balance, economic growth, and the 'Make in India' initiative.
articles.imageDescription: Cargo ships and containers at a port, symbolizing international trade.
articles.trivia: India aims to achieve goods and services exports of $2 trillion by 2030.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economic and Social Development
prelimsSyllabus.area: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Foreign Trade Policy and its impact

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands to Integrate More Healthcare Providers and Services
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) announced the further expansion of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to integrate a wider network of healthcare providers and services.
• The mission aims to create a national digital health ecosystem, enabling seamless access to health records, teleconsultations, and digital prescriptions.
• New partnerships with private hospitals, diagnostic labs, and health-tech startups are being forged to onboard them onto the ABDM platform.
• Focus on enhancing interoperability, data security, and patient privacy within the digital health infrastructure.
• This expansion is crucial for achieving universal health coverage and making healthcare more accessible and efficient across India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Awareness in the fields of IT
prelimsPoints: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) is expanding to integrate more healthcare providers.
It aims to create a national digital health ecosystem.
The mission facilitates access to health records, teleconsultations, and digital prescriptions.
NHA is the implementing agency for ABDM.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in revolutionizing healthcare delivery and access in India.
Analyze the challenges involved in building a robust and secure digital health ecosystem, including data privacy concerns, digital literacy, and infrastructure gaps.
Evaluate how the expansion of ABDM can contribute to achieving universal health coverage and improving public health outcomes across diverse regions of India.
articles.imageDescription: Digital interface showing health data and a network of healthcare providers.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) is the world's largest government-funded health assurance scheme.

articles.headline: New Guidelines Issued for Smart Cities Mission to Accelerate Project Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs released updated guidelines for the Smart Cities Mission to expedite project implementation and enhance efficiency.
• The revised framework emphasizes citizen-centric urban planning, sustainable infrastructure development, and technology integration for improved governance.
• Focus on leveraging public-private partnerships (PPPs) and innovative financing models to overcome implementation bottlenecks.
• Encourages cities to adopt a 'living lab' approach for piloting new urban solutions and sharing best practices across the country.
• The aim is to make Indian cities more livable, sustainable, and economically vibrant, aligning with the broader vision of urban development.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Development processes and the development industry – the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: New guidelines issued for the Smart Cities Mission.
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry.
Emphasizes citizen-centric planning, sustainable infrastructure, and technology integration.
Encourages public-private partnerships and a 'living lab' approach.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the progress and challenges of the Smart Cities Mission in India since its inception.
Discuss how the new guidelines aim to accelerate project implementation and address existing bottlenecks, focusing on citizen participation and sustainable development.
Analyze the role of technology and innovative financing models in transforming Indian cities into smart, livable, and economically vibrant urban centers.
articles.imageDescription: Modern smart city skyline with digital overlays showing data and connectivity.
articles.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 with the objective of promoting cities that provide core infrastructure, a clean and sustainable environment, and a decent quality of life to their citizens.

highlights: Government unveils 'Vision India 2047' roadmap for achieving developed nation status by India's centenary of independence.
India hosts the 3rd Quad Leaders' Summit, reinforcing cooperation on Indo-Pacific security and critical technologies.
ISRO successfully conducts a crucial test flight for its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology demonstrator.
Supreme Court begins hearing petitions challenging key provisions of the recently enacted Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
RBI maintains key interest rates, projecting stable inflation outlook amidst global economic uncertainties.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding 'Vision India 2047':
1. It aims for India to become a developed nation by 2047.
2. Green growth and digital economy are among its key pillars.
3. The document emphasizes strengthening federal cooperation for effective implementation.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. 'Vision India 2047' indeed aims for India to become a developed nation by 2047, focuses on pillars like green growth and digital economy, and stresses federal cooperation for implementation.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following countries are members of the Quad grouping?
1. India
2. Japan
3. South Korea
4. Australia
5. United States

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) is a strategic dialogue between four countries: India, the United States, Japan, and Australia. South Korea is not a member of the Quad.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, consider the following statements:
1. It seeks to protect the digital personal data of individuals.
2. The Act establishes a Data Protection Board of India.
3. The Supreme Court has already upheld all provisions of the Act without any challenges.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The DPDP Act aims to protect digital personal data and establishes a Data Protection Board. Statement 3 is incorrect as the Supreme Court has just commenced hearing petitions challenging various provisions of the Act, indicating ongoing legal challenges.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Varuna' naval exercise is conducted bilaterally between India and which other country?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Exercise 'Varuna' is a bilateral naval exercise conducted between the Indian Navy and the French Navy. It is a key component of the strategic partnership between India and France.

prelimsMocks.question: The Finance Commission in India is constituted under which Article of the Constitution?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Finance Commission is a constitutional body in India, constituted by the President of India under Article 280 of the Constitution. Its primary role is to make recommendations on the distribution of tax revenues between the Union and the States, and among the States themselves.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the 'Vision India 2047' blueprint for economic transformation. What are the key challenges and opportunities in achieving the goal of a developed nation, particularly concerning federal cooperation and resource mobilization?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; GS2: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Vision India 2047's objectives (developed nation by 2047, green growth, digital economy). Discuss opportunities (demographic dividend, technological advancements, growing market). Detail challenges: fiscal constraints, regional disparities, climate change impacts, geopolitical uncertainties. Emphasize the role of robust federal cooperation and efficient resource mobilization (public, private, foreign investment) as crucial for successful implementation.

mainsMocks.question: Examine the evolving role of the Quad grouping in shaping the Indo-Pacific security architecture. Discuss India's strategic interests within the Quad and the challenges it faces in balancing security cooperation with broader regional inclusivity.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Define Quad's evolution from an informal dialogue to a significant grouping. Discuss its objectives: free and open Indo-Pacific, maritime security, critical technologies, climate action. Analyze India's interests (countering China, securing trade routes, tech access, disaster relief). Address challenges: balancing relations with non-Quad nations, avoiding perception of an 'Asian NATO', ensuring economic engagement alongside security, and maintaining strategic autonomy.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the increasing vulnerabilities of India's critical infrastructure to cyber threats. Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives, such as the new guidelines issued by NCIIPC, in safeguarding these vital assets, and suggest further measures for enhancing cyber resilience.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, basics of cyber security; Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the concept of critical infrastructure and its importance. Detail various cyber threats (ransomware, state-sponsored attacks, data breaches). Explain NCIIPC's role and the new guidelines (multi-layered security, audits, threat intelligence). Evaluate their effectiveness (positive steps but implementation challenges). Suggest further measures: public-private partnership, capacity building, international cooperation, legal framework updates, R&D in indigenous cyber solutions, public awareness campaigns.

quickBytes.text: The 16th Finance Commission will make recommendations for the period 2026-2031.
India's National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for 10 MTPA production capacity by 2030.
The Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act was passed in 2023.
ISRO successfully tested its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
The Western Ghats are a UNESCO World Heritage site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises six members, three from RBI and three external.
India's services sector is the largest contributor to its GDP, accounting for over 50%.
The 'Poshan Abhiyaan' (National Nutrition Mission) was launched in 2018.
NCIIPC stands for National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre.
Article 324 of the Indian Constitution deals with the Election Commission of India.

GS1.headline: IMD Issues Advisory for Extreme Weather Events, Emphasizes Disaster Preparedness
GS1.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued a nationwide advisory, warning of increased likelihood of extreme weather events in the coming months.
• The advisory highlighted potential for intense rainfall, heatwaves, and localized storms, attributing the trends to ongoing climate change impacts.
• Emphasized the need for robust disaster preparedness measures, including early warning systems, evacuation plans, and resilient infrastructure.
• Urged state governments and disaster management agencies to enhance coordination and public awareness campaigns.
• The IMD also stressed the importance of sustainable urban planning and water management to mitigate the impact of such events.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: IMD issued an advisory on extreme weather events.
It warned of intense rainfall, heatwaves, and localized storms.
The advisory emphasizes disaster preparedness and early warning systems.
IMD is the primary agency for meteorological observations and forecasting in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing frequency and intensity of extreme weather events in India and their attribution to global climate change.
Analyze the effectiveness of India's current disaster preparedness and early warning systems in mitigating the impact of such events.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for enhancing disaster resilience at local and national levels, including infrastructure development, community engagement, and policy reforms.
GS1.imageDescription: Weather map showing various extreme weather indicators over India.
GS1.trivia: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) was established in 1875 and is an agency of the Ministry of Earth Sciences.

GS2.headline: India Hosts 3rd Quad Leaders' Summit, Reinforcing Indo-Pacific Security and Tech Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• Leaders from India, the United States, Japan, and Australia convened in New Delhi for the third in-person Quad Summit.
• Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in maritime security, counter-terrorism, critical and emerging technologies, and resilient supply chains.
• A new 'Quad Infrastructure Partnership' was announced to support sustainable and transparent infrastructure development in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Emphasized commitment to a free, open, inclusive, and resilient Indo-Pacific, upholding international law and norms.
• Agreed on joint initiatives for climate action, health security, and humanitarian assistance/disaster relief.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: The 3rd Quad Leaders' Summit was hosted by India in New Delhi.
Quad members include India, USA, Japan, and Australia.
A 'Quad Infrastructure Partnership' was announced.
Focus areas include maritime security, critical technologies, and climate action.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Quad grouping for India's strategic interests in the Indo-Pacific region and its role in global governance.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for the Quad in balancing security concerns with economic cooperation and regional inclusivity.
Evaluate the potential impact of the 'Quad Infrastructure Partnership' on regional development and geopolitical dynamics.
GS2.imageDescription: Leaders of the Quad nations shaking hands at the summit venue in New Delhi.
GS2.trivia: The idea of the Quad was first mooted by Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe in 2007.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: International Institutions, agencies and fora, their structure and mandate
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Important International institutions, agencies and fora – their structure, mandate

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Begins Hearing Petitions Challenging Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court commenced hearing a batch of petitions challenging various provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023.
• Petitioners argue that certain clauses infringe upon fundamental rights, particularly the right to privacy and freedom of speech and expression.
• Key points of contention include the wide powers granted to the Data Protection Board, exemptions for government agencies, and the definition of 'personal data'.
• The government is expected to defend the Act, emphasizing its role in safeguarding citizen data and promoting digital economy.
• The outcome of the hearing is crucial for shaping India's data governance framework and digital rights.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
Fundamental Rights
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act was passed in 2023.
The Supreme Court is hearing challenges to the Act.
Arguments revolve around the right to privacy and freedom of speech.
The Data Protection Board is a key institution under the Act.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, that are under judicial scrutiny, particularly concerning the balance between national security and individual privacy.
Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's potential ruling on India's digital economy, data localization, and the rights of digital citizens.
Analyze the role of the Data Protection Board and its independence in safeguarding personal data in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Education Launches 'Skill India Digital 2.0' for Enhanced Vocational Training
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education, in collaboration with the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship, launched 'Skill India Digital 2.0'.
• This upgraded platform aims to provide a comprehensive digital ecosystem for skill development, vocational training, and employment linkages.
• It integrates various government and private sector skilling initiatives, offering personalized learning paths, industry-relevant courses, and digital certifications.
• Focuses on bridging the skill gap in emerging sectors like AI, data science, green jobs, and advanced manufacturing.
• The platform also facilitates direct connection between skilled individuals and employers, enhancing job placement opportunities across the country.
gsSubTopics: Human Resources
Development processes and the development industry – the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Skill India Digital 2.0 is launched by the Ministry of Education and Ministry of Skill Development.
It is a digital ecosystem for skill development and employment.
The platform offers personalized learning paths and digital certifications.
It aims to bridge the skill gap in emerging sectors.
mainsPoints: Discuss how digital platforms like 'Skill India Digital 2.0' can revolutionize vocational training and address the challenges of unemployment and underemployment in India.
Analyze the role of public-private partnerships in making skill development initiatives more effective and industry-relevant.
Examine the potential of 'Skill India Digital 2.0' to foster a culture of lifelong learning and adapt the workforce to future job market demands.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized logo of Skill India Digital with interconnected digital icons.
GS2.trivia: The 'Skill India Mission' was launched in 2015 to create convergence across various schemes and programs related to skill training.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Reforms in Election Funding Transparency
GS2.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee submitted its report recommending significant reforms to enhance transparency in election funding in India.
• Key recommendations include bringing all political parties under the ambit of the Right to Information (RTI) Act for greater accountability.
• Suggested capping anonymous donations and mandating disclosure of donors for contributions above a certain threshold.
• The committee also called for strengthening the Election Commission of India's (ECI) powers to regulate political finance and ensure compliance.
• The recommendations aim to address concerns regarding black money in elections and ensure a level playing field for all political parties.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: A parliamentary panel recommended reforms in election funding.
Key recommendations include bringing political parties under RTI Act.
It also suggested capping anonymous donations and strengthening ECI powers.
The aim is to increase transparency in political finance.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the current framework of election funding in India and the challenges it poses to democratic principles and fair elections.
Discuss the feasibility and potential impact of the parliamentary panel's recommendations, such as bringing political parties under the RTI Act, on improving transparency and accountability.
Suggest further reforms that could effectively curb the influence of black money in elections and ensure a level playing field for all political contestants.
GS2.imageDescription: Parliament House of India with a ballot box overlay.
GS2.trivia: The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body established under Article 324 of the Constitution.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Substantial Aid to Developing Nations for Climate Resilience at UN Forum
GS2.explainer:
• India announced a significant financial commitment to support climate resilience and adaptation projects in developing countries at a United Nations forum.
• The pledge underscores India's growing leadership in global climate action and its commitment to South-South cooperation.
• Funds will be directed towards initiatives like early warning systems, climate-resilient agriculture, and renewable energy deployment in vulnerable nations.
• The announcement comes ahead of the upcoming COP29, signaling India's proactive stance on climate finance for developing countries.
• Emphasized the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in climate negotiations.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure and mandate
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: India pledged climate aid to developing nations at a UN forum.
The aid focuses on climate resilience and adaptation projects.
It highlights South-South cooperation and India's leadership in climate action.
The principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities (CBDR-RC) was emphasized.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India's climate aid pledge for developing nations in the context of its foreign policy objectives and global climate leadership.
Discuss the challenges in mobilizing and effectively utilizing climate finance for adaptation and resilience in vulnerable countries.
Evaluate India's role in advocating for the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in international climate negotiations.
GS2.imageDescription: Globe with hands reaching out, symbolizing international cooperation on climate change.
GS2.trivia: The 'Green Climate Fund' (GCF) is a fund established within the framework of the UNFCCC to assist developing countries in adaptation and mitigation practices.

GS2.headline: Government Launches Nationwide Campaign to Combat Malnutrition Among Children
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched a comprehensive nationwide campaign, 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan', to address malnutrition among children.
• The campaign focuses on improving nutritional outcomes through targeted interventions, awareness generation, and community participation.
• Initiatives include promoting exclusive breastfeeding, complementary feeding, micronutrient supplementation, and early childhood care.
• Leverages the Anganwadi network and healthcare workers for effective last-mile delivery of services and counseling to mothers.
• Aims to reduce stunting, wasting, and anemia among children under five, aligning with Sustainable Development Goal 2 (Zero Hunger).
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Hunger and poverty
prelimsPoints: The 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan' campaign aims to combat child malnutrition.
It is launched by the Ministry of Women and Child Development.
The campaign focuses on breastfeeding, complementary feeding, and micronutrient supplementation.
It utilizes the Anganwadi network for service delivery.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional causes and consequences of child malnutrition in India and its implications for human capital development and economic growth.
Analyze the effectiveness of government initiatives like 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan' and the Anganwadi system in addressing the challenge of malnutrition.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including inter-sectoral convergence and community engagement, to achieve the Sustainable Development Goal of zero hunger in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A healthy child smiling, with a graphic depicting balanced nutrition.
GS2.trivia: The 'Poshan Abhiyaan' (National Nutrition Mission) was launched in 2018 to improve nutritional outcomes for children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economic and Social Development
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government schemes for health and nutrition

GS2.headline: 16th Finance Commission Holds Crucial Meeting on Centre-State Fiscal Relations
GS2.explainer:
• The 16th Finance Commission held a significant meeting to deliberate on the framework for horizontal and vertical devolution of taxes between the Centre and States.
• Discussions focused on equitable distribution of resources, addressing fiscal imbalances, and incentivizing states for performance on various parameters.
• The Commission is tasked with making recommendations for the period 2026-2031, impacting state finances and development priorities.
• Key areas under review include criteria for resource sharing, grants-in-aid, and disaster relief funding mechanisms.
• State representatives presented their proposals, advocating for increased fiscal autonomy and greater share in central taxes.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Finance Commission
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: The 16th Finance Commission is deliberating on Centre-State fiscal relations.
It will make recommendations for the period 2026-2031.
The Commission focuses on horizontal and vertical devolution of taxes.
Finance Commission is a constitutional body under Article 280.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional mandate and critical role of the Finance Commission in shaping fiscal federalism in India.
Analyze the key challenges faced by the 16th Finance Commission in ensuring equitable and efficient resource devolution between the Centre and States, considering evolving economic realities.
Evaluate the potential impact of the Commission's recommendations on state finances, development priorities, and the overall trajectory of India's federal structure.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian rupee currency notes with a graph showing economic growth, symbolizing fiscal policy.
GS2.trivia: The first Finance Commission was constituted in 1951 under the chairmanship of K.C. Neogy.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands to Integrate More Healthcare Providers and Services
GS2.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) announced the further expansion of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to integrate a wider network of healthcare providers and services.
• The mission aims to create a national digital health ecosystem, enabling seamless access to health records, teleconsultations, and digital prescriptions.
• New partnerships with private hospitals, diagnostic labs, and health-tech startups are being forged to onboard them onto the ABDM platform.
• Focus on enhancing interoperability, data security, and patient privacy within the digital health infrastructure.
• This expansion is crucial for achieving universal health coverage and making healthcare more accessible and efficient across India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Awareness in the fields of IT
prelimsPoints: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) is expanding to integrate more healthcare providers.
It aims to create a national digital health ecosystem.
The mission facilitates access to health records, teleconsultations, and digital prescriptions.
NHA is the implementing agency for ABDM.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in revolutionizing healthcare delivery and access in India.
Analyze the challenges involved in building a robust and secure digital health ecosystem, including data privacy concerns, digital literacy, and infrastructure gaps.
Evaluate how the expansion of ABDM can contribute to achieving universal health coverage and improving public health outcomes across diverse regions of India.
GS2.imageDescription: Digital interface showing health data and a network of healthcare providers.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) is the world's largest government-funded health assurance scheme.

GS2.headline: New Guidelines Issued for Smart Cities Mission to Accelerate Project Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs released updated guidelines for the Smart Cities Mission to expedite project implementation and enhance efficiency.
• The revised framework emphasizes citizen-centric urban planning, sustainable infrastructure development, and technology integration for improved governance.
• Focus on leveraging public-private partnerships (PPPs) and innovative financing models to overcome implementation bottlenecks.
• Encourages cities to adopt a 'living lab' approach for piloting new urban solutions and sharing best practices across the country.
• The aim is to make Indian cities more livable, sustainable, and economically vibrant, aligning with the broader vision of urban development.

Development processes and the development industry – the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
